item.title: Gov. Jared Polis commutes Tina Peters' prison sentence
item.description: Colorado's governor on Friday announced that he is commuting the sentence of former Mesa County Clerk Tina Peters.

item.title: Which college majors offer the best long-term return on investment?
item.description: College grads outearn people without a degree within 15 years, even after paying for tuition, study finds.

item.title: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
item.description: Health officials have identified at least 10 confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.

item.title: Prominent N.Y. synagogue targeted in alleged terror plot, prosecutors say
item.description: An Iraqi national allegedly plotted to carry out terror attacks in the U.S., including at a prominent synagogue in New York, prosecutors said Friday

item.title: Democratic Rep. Steve Cohen drops reelection bid in wake of Tennessee redistricting
item.description: Democratic Rep. Steve Cohen of Tennessee ended his reelection campaign on Friday. The decision comes after the state's GOP lawmakers voted to split up the Memphis congressional district he's represented for almost 20 years.

item.title: The Knowledge | Sunday on 60 Minutes
item.description: London's iconic black cabs are about to compete with AI-powered, autonomous taxis. But the city's cabbies aren't ready to hand over their keys. Anderson Cooper reports, Sunday on 60 Minutes.

item.title: Cuba's biggest threat to U.S. is collapse, says former defense secretary
item.description: In an interview with "Face the Nation," Gates said another mass exodus from Cuba is the "biggest risk."

item.title: Robert Gates says Cuba's biggest threat to U.S. is its potential collapse
item.description: Former Defense Secretary Robert Gates told "Face the Nation" moderator Margaret Brennan that the biggest threat Cuba poses to the U.S. is its potential collapse. "The biggest risk is that we end up with another Mariel evacuation from Cuba that has tens of thousands of Cubans heading to the United States out of desperation," Gates said in an interview airing Sunday.

item.title: How will Jerome Powell be remembered as he exits as Fed chair?
item.description: As Powell steps down after more than eight years leading the Federal Reserve, economists say he helped steer the U.S. through historic shocks but misread inflation.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (May 17): "By Design"
item.description: "Sunday Morning" presents its annual edition touching on all aspects of design, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Pentagon's rush to counter drone threat may pose safety risks, specialist says
item.description: The safety specialist's warning appeared in a memo describing how a mini-drone had detonated and injured an Army Special Forces soldier.

item.title: Iraqi national plotted terror attacks in U.S., officials say
item.description: Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood Al-Saadi is facing federal charges for allegedly plotting several terror attacks in the U.S., including targeting a Jewish institution in New York. CBS News' Anna Schecter and Jake Rosen report.

item.title: Iran says it "cannot trust" U.S. in negotiations as Trump says "we control" strait
item.description: Speaking to reporters on Air Force One, President Trump also claimed to have "wiped out" Iran's armed forces.

item.title: 5 tourists killed in cave diving accident; 4 victims still not found
item.description: The University of Genoa said the victims included a marine biology professor, her daughter and two young researchers.

item.title: Jerome Powell's legacy as Federal Reserve chair
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's last day comes after a tense tenure through a pandemic, economic turmoil and a rocky relationship with President Trump. CBS News' Emily Pandise reports.

item.title: Soldier says ICE released wife after she spent a month in detention
item.description: Army Sgt. 1st Class Jose Serrano says his wife, Deisy Rivera Ortega, is back home after U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement detained her for a month. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.

item.title: New dinosaur weighing as much as 9 elephants identified by scientists
item.description: The long-necked herbivore is the largest ever found in Southeast Asia, researchers said.

item.title: Pirro threatens charges against parents if their teens violate local curfew
item.description: In a move aimed at curbing the growing problem of "teen takeovers," D.C. U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro is threatening to bring charges against parents if their teens violate the local curfew.

item.title: Democratic Rep. Steve Cohen drops reelection bid after redistricting
item.description: Democratic Rep. Steve Cohen of Tennessee announced Friday that he's retiring from Congress at the end of this term after his district in Memphis was redrawn.

item.title: Why is Trump talking about action on Cuba and what could that look like?
item.description: Experts don't expect military action soon. But actual regime change is complicated.

item.title: Extended interview: Tim Cook
item.description: In this web exclusive, Tim Cook, the CEO of Apple, talks with "Sunday Morning" correspondent David Pogue (author of "Apple: The First 50 Years") to discuss the company's first half-century and its constant focus on "the next thing." He also talks about the vision of Steve Jobs, whose return to Apple in 1997 reinvigorated the company.

item.title: Extended interview: Jack Schlossberg
item.description: Jack Schlossberg, the 33-year-old grandson of President John F. Kennedy, is running for Congress in New York's 12th District. In this extended interview, he talks with correspondent Mo Rocca about his family; the skepticism and apathy of many voters for politics today; and why he is pursuing public office.

item.title: Extended interview: Marc Shaiman
item.description: In this web exclusive, composer Marc Shaiman, a Tony Award-winner for "Hairspray," and a seven-time Oscar nominee, talks with Tracy Smith about his career; his new memoir, "Never Mind the Happy: Showbiz Stories From a Sore Winner"; and his collaboration and friendship with Rob Reiner.

item.title: Extended interview: Jack Schlossberg at home
item.description: Jack Schlossberg, the 33-year-old grandson of President John F. Kennedy, is running for Congress in New York's 12th District. In this extended interview, he talks with correspondent Mo Rocca about his family's support for his campaign; addressing the Trump administration's attacks on his grandfather's legacy; and about his recovery from a serious back injury.

item.title: Extended interview: Rose Byrne
item.description: In this web exclusive, Rose Byrne, an Oscar nominee for best actress for her performance in Mary Bronstein's "If I Had Legs I'd Kick You," talks with Tracy Smith about her response to the character of Linda, a woman at the end of her rope. She also talks about her early years in Hollywood, including her friendship with fellow Australian Heath Ledger, and her experience playing comedy with "Bridesmaids."

item.title: Extended interview: Itzhak Perlman
item.description: In this web exclusive, famed violinist Itzhak Perlman talks with CBS News chief medical correspondent Dr. Jonathan LaPook about his experience after contracting polio as a child, several years before the development of a polio vaccine, and the obstacles in life to which he has had to adjust because of his disability. He has advice for those who question taking the vaccine. He also talks about the effect of music on the brain, and how he wishes to be remembered.

item.title: Extended interview: Stephen A. Smith
item.description: In this web exclusive, the host of ESPN's "First Take" talks with "Sunday Morning" national correspondent Robert Costa about being an authentic (albeit at times controversial) voice on sports (and, now, politics).

item.title: Extended interview: Jacob Elordi
item.description: In this web exclusive, Australian actor Jacob Elordi talks with Tracy Smith about his Oscar-nominated performance as the Creature in Guillermo del Toro's "Frankenstein"; playing Elvis Presley in Sophia Coppola's "Priscilla"; and taking on the role of Heathcliff in "Wuthering Heights."

item.title: Extended Interview: Don Henley
item.description: Singer-songwriter Don Henley reflects on the Eagles' decades-long career, the larger than life legacy of his late bandmate Glenn Frey, and the emotional moment he saw Frey's son Deacon perform in his father's place. He also emphasizes the importance of connecting with fans, saying "music is medicine. And people need some medicine right now."

item.title: Extended Interview: Ilia Malinin
item.description: In this web exclusive, figure skating superstar Ilia Malinin talks with correspondent Jonathan Vigliotti in advance of the Winter Olympics. He talks about the artistry of his routines; having his parents (former Olympic skaters Roman Skorniakov and Tatiana Malinina) as coaches; and landing a quad axel.
